steps.title: Count the number of doublings
steps.content: From 1640 to 1960 is $1960-1640=320$ years. With doubling every 80 years, the count is $\tfrac{320}{80}=4$ doublings.

steps.title: Solve for the initial population
steps.content: Let $P_0$ be the 1640 population. Then $P_0\cdot 2^4=192{,}000$. $$P_0=\frac{192{,}000}{16}=12{,}000.$$ The answer is 12000.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $12000$; substituting or interpreting it in the original condition satisfies the requirement, so $12000$ is the value to enter.
